acceptance_targets: [chapter_04_dialogue_001] Pass if the revised dialogue at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:49, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:159 gives each named speaker at least one line that remains attributable without a tag because its diction and rhythm are uniquely hers. Fail if those lines can still be swapped between Vero, Marta, Nessa, Ruthie, and Inez with little or no adjustment.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised dialogue at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:49, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:159 gives each named speaker at least one line that remains attributable without a tag because its diction and rhythm are uniquely hers. Fail if those lines can still be swapped between Vero, Marta, Nessa, Ruthie, and Inez with little or no adjustment.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:159, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:177 all land in the same polished, quotable aphoristic rhythm despite belonging to Vero, Marta, Nessa, and Ruthie.
findings.problem: The ward women are vivid in description, but too many of their spoken lines share the same neatly turned, authorially polished cadence. That blurs idiolect separation and especially underserves Aunt Vero, whose style-bible syntax should meander, double back, and then land hard rather than staying in short epigram mode. The result is that Inez's controlled sharpness bleeds into multiple side voices instead of each woman sounding shaped by her own labor pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the exchanges anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:49, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:159 so each speaker carries a distinct lexical field and sentence rhythm. Give Vero at least one looping, baiting sentence with bodily or fermentation texture; let Marta stay stove-and-supper practical; let Nessa's syntax feel tied to sewing, mourning, and baby-handling; let Ruthie sound interrupted by childcare and iron work rather than like another polished wit machine.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on dialogue realism and voice separation: idiolect fidelity, leverage shifts, pressure cadence, anti-transcript behavior, natural colloquial texture (contractions/interruptions/slang when character-true), and pressure-true profanity usage per aesthetic risk policy.
revision_pass.label: dialogue/idiolect/cadence fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
